name: Wycliffe Gordon
abstract: Wycliffe A. Gordon (born May 29, 1967) is an American jazz trombonist, arranger, composer, band leader, and music educator at the collegiate-conservatory level.

interviews.title: Wycliffe Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 17, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Adrian Cunningham
about_quotes.evidence: Wycliffe Gordon is one of my biggest influences just in terms of a human being... I’ve been in his quintet for, what, six or seven years now
about_quotes.doc_title: Adrian Cunningham interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 31, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: John Fedchock
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve been working with him over the last six years now I guess.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Fedchock interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 28, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Nat Hentoff
about_quotes.evidence: I can tell Wycliffe Gordon, because when he plays trombone that’s the whole history of jazz in his way of doing it... I think there is not yet enough strongly individual voices... somebody like Wycliffe Gordon is wonderful. He’s part of the living jazz tradition.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nat Hentoff interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Cullum
about_quotes.evidence: it’s one of the reasons that I’m here at IAJE. We would love to see, as time goes by, traditional jazz or earlier forms of jazz sort of like Wycliffe Gordon was alluding to some of these things today
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Cullum interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Segal
about_quotes.evidence: Like we have Wycliffe Gordon coming in with Columbia College. He’ll play there a week in residence and tutor the students, and then play here on the weekend, and we share the costs with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Segal — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: I enjoyed almost as much, in a different way, meeting Wycliffe Gordon... we got talking a little bit... He brought me a CD that he did with somebody else... I enjoyed it. It was a nice week.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001
